description: Forces the CPU to always operate at 100% of its performance state, eliminating clock speed ramp-up delays at the cost of higher idle power draw.

educationalContext.howItWorks: By default, Windows allows the CPU to idle at as low as 5% of its performance state (e.g., 400 MHz on a 4 GHz chip). Setting the minimum to 100% prevents downclocking, so the CPU is always ready to respond instantly.
educationalContext.pros: Zero latency to reach full CPU frequency. Eliminates 'wake-up' stutters at the start of frames in CPU-bound games. Measurable improvement in minimum frametimes.
educationalContext.cons: Significantly higher idle power and heat. CPU will not throttle down when idle, increasing electricity use by 10–30W. Not recommended for laptops or thermally constrained systems.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PROCTHROTTLEMIN 100; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PROCTHROTTLEMIN 5; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Enable Ultimate Performance Power Plan
description: Unhides and activates the Ultimate Performance power plan — Microsoft's highest performance preset.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows ships with a hidden 'Ultimate Performance' plan that disables all power-saving features. This command enables it.
educationalContext.pros: Maximum CPU/GPU performance. Eliminates all power-saving latency. Best for desktop gaming rigs.
educationalContext.cons: Higher power consumption and heat. Not suitable for laptops on battery.

name: Increase IRPStackSize
description: Increases the I/O Request Packet stack size for better network file sharing performance.

educationalContext.howItWorks: IRPStackSize controls how many I/O request packets the network stack can queue. Default of 15 is too low for many NAS/SMB setups.
educationalContext.pros: Faster SMB file transfers. Eliminates 'insufficient server storage' errors.
educationalContext.cons: Negligible additional memory usage. Safe on all systems.

name: Enable DNS over HTTPS (DoH)
description: Enables DNS over HTTPS to encrypt DNS queries and prevent ISP snooping.

educationalContext.howItWorks: DoH wraps DNS queries in HTTPS, preventing ISPs and network observers from seeing what domains you visit.
educationalContext.pros: Encrypted DNS queries. ISP can't see your browsing domains. Prevents DNS hijacking.
educationalContext.cons: Requires Windows 11 or higher. Slightly higher latency on first lookup.

name: Disable Wi-Fi Sense
description: Disables Wi-Fi Sense that shares your network credentials with contacts.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Wi-Fi Sense automatically shares your Wi-Fi passwords with Outlook/Skype/Facebook contacts.
educationalContext.pros: Your Wi-Fi credentials stay private. No auto-sharing with contacts.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the convenience of auto-sharing Wi-Fi access with trusted contacts.

name: Disable Startup Application Delay
description: Removes the artificial delay Windows adds before launching startup apps.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows adds a 10-second delay before launching startup programs. This removes it.
educationalContext.pros: Startup apps launch immediately at login. Desktop is ready faster.
educationalContext.cons: Higher CPU usage immediately after boot. System may feel briefly sluggish on slow PCs.

name: Speed Up Shutdown Time
description: Reduces WaitToKillServiceTimeout and HungAppTimeout for faster shutdowns.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows waits up to 5 seconds for services and hung apps to close. This reduces it to 2 seconds.
educationalContext.pros: Shutdown and restart complete noticeably faster.
educationalContext.cons: Apps with unsaved data may not get enough time to save. Most apps auto-save.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control' -Name 'WaitToKillServiceTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'HungAppTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'WaitToKillAppTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control' -Name 'WaitToKillServiceTimeout' -Value '5000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'HungAppTimeout' -Value '5000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'WaitToKillAppTimeout' -Value '20000' -Type String -Force

name: Disable Advertising ID
description: Disables your unique advertising identifier that Microsoft uses to track you across apps.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows assigns a unique advertising ID to your profile for targeted ads in apps and browsers.
educationalContext.pros: Apps can no longer track you via Microsoft's advertising ID.
educationalContext.cons: You may see less relevant ads in free Microsoft Store apps.

name: Disable Location Tracking
description: Disables Windows location services and prevents apps from accessing your location.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects location data via GPS, Wi-Fi, and IP geolocation. This disables it system-wide.
educationalContext.pros: No app or Microsoft service can access your physical location.
educationalContext.cons: Weather, Maps, and Find My Device won't work correctly.

name: Disable Clipboard Cloud Sync
description: Prevents clipboard contents from being synced to the cloud across devices.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows syncs clipboard data (including copied passwords) across your Microsoft account devices.
educationalContext.pros: Your clipboard contents (passwords, sensitive text) stay local.
educationalContext.cons: You can't paste between your Windows devices via the cloud.

name: Flush DNS Cache
description: Clears the local DNS resolver cache to fix stale DNS entries and resolve connectivity issues.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Clears all cached DNS lookups. The next connection will do a fresh DNS resolution.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes websites not loading after DNS changes. Resolves stale cache entries.
educationalContext.cons: First visit to each site will be slightly slower (one DNS lookup). Cache rebuilds in seconds.

execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'DNS cache is rebuilt automatically as you browse.'

name: Reset Winsock Catalog
description: Resets the Winsock catalog to fix network connectivity issues caused by corrupted LSP entries.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Winsock is Windows' network socket API. Corrupted entries from VPNs or security software can break all networking.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes 'no internet' issues, DNS failures, and corrupted network stacks.
educationalContext.cons: Requires a reboot. Some VPN/proxy software may need reinstalling.

execution.code: netsh winsock reset; netsh int ip reset
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Winsock reset cannot be explicitly reversed. Network will reconfigure on reboot.'

name: Disable Remote Desktop
description: Disables Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) — a major attack vector if left enabled.

educationalContext.howItWorks: RDP allows remote access to your PC. If enabled, attackers can brute-force the login from the internet.
educationalContext.pros: Closes a common entry point for ransomware and unauthorized access.
educationalContext.cons: You can't remotely control this PC. Use alternatives like RustDesk or Parsec.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' -Name 'fDenyTSConnections' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Disable-NetFirewallRule -DisplayGroup 'Remote Desktop' -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' -Name 'fDenyTSConnections' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Enable-NetFirewallRule -DisplayGroup 'Remote Desktop' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable SMBv1 Protocol
description: Disables the outdated SMBv1 protocol used by WannaCry and other ransomware.

educationalContext.howItWorks: SMBv1 has critical vulnerabilities (EternalBlue exploit). Modern networks use SMBv2/v3.
educationalContext.pros: Closes the attack vector used by WannaCry, NotPetya, and other major exploits.
educationalContext.cons: Very old printers or NAS devices (pre-2012) may not work. All modern devices use SMBv2+.

name: Block Cortana
description: Disables Cortana and prevents it from running in the background.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Cortana runs as a background process even when not in use, consuming RAM and sending data to Microsoft.
educationalContext.pros: Saves 50-100MB RAM. No more background data collection by Cortana.
educationalContext.cons: Voice search and Cortana features will not work.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortana' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortanaAboveLock' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortana' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortanaAboveLock' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Background Apps
description: Prevents UWP/Store apps from running in the background and consuming resources.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows allows Store apps to run background tasks for live tiles, notifications, and updates.
educationalContext.pros: Reclaims CPU, RAM, and battery used by background UWP apps. Reduces I/O noise.
educationalContext.cons: Store app notifications and live tiles won't update until you open the app.

name: Disable Activity History
description: Disables Activity History which tracks your recent docs, clipboard, and run history.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows Timeline tracks app usage, file access, and browsing history. This disables it entirely.
educationalContext.pros: Stops Windows from recording your recent activity. No data sent to Microsoft Timeline.
educationalContext.cons: You lose Windows Timeline/Task View history and cross-device activity resume.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableActivityFeed' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'PublishUserActivities' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'UploadUserActivities' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableActivityFeed' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'PublishUserActivities' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'UploadUserActivities' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Enable 'End Task' in Taskbar Right-Click
description: Adds 'End Task' to the taskbar right-click context menu (Win11 23H2+).

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 23H2+ has a hidden 'End Task' option in the taskbar right-click menu. This enables it.
educationalContext.pros: Kill frozen apps directly from the taskbar without opening Task Manager.
educationalContext.cons: None. This is a hidden QoL feature that Microsoft should have enabled by default.

name: Disable Windows Copilot
description: Completely disables Windows Copilot AI assistant and removes its taskbar button.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows Copilot runs as a sidebar AI that sends your queries to Microsoft. This blocks it via Group Policy.
educationalContext.pros: No AI telemetry. Removes the Copilot button from taskbar. Saves background resources.
educationalContext.cons: You lose access to the Copilot AI assistant in Windows.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot' -Name 'TurnOffWindowsCopilot' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' -Name 'ShowCopilotButton' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Restore Classic Right-Click Menu (Win11)
description: Brings back the full classic context menu on right-click in Windows 11 instead of the simplified version.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 replaced the full context menu with a truncated version. This registry key forces the legacy menu.
educationalContext.pros: All context menu items visible immediately. No more 'Show more options' click.
educationalContext.cons: Loses the modern rounded context menu design. Requires Explorer restart.

name: Disable Sticky Keys Popup
description: Disables the Sticky Keys popup when pressing Shift 5 times — essential for gaming.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Pressing Shift 5 times triggers a Sticky Keys accessibility prompt. This disables that shortcut.
educationalContext.pros: No more annoying popups during gaming when spamming Shift.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the Shift×5 shortcut to enable Sticky Keys (still accessible via Settings).

name: Disable IPv6
description: Disables IPv6 on all network adapters. Can fix connectivity issues on IPv4-only networks.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the IPv6 protocol stack. Most consumer ISPs still primarily use IPv4.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes DNS leaks in some VPN configs. Can resolve connectivity issues on IPv4-only networks.
educationalContext.cons: Breaks IPv6-only services. Some modern apps and services are moving to IPv6.

execution.code: Get-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue | Disable-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters' -Name 'DisabledComponents' -Value 255 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Get-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue | Enable-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters' -Name 'DisabledComponents' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Windows Platform Binary Table
description: Blocks OEMs from force-installing software via UEFI firmware on every boot.

educationalContext.howItWorks: WPBT lets motherboard vendors embed executables in UEFI that Windows runs automatically at boot.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents vendor bloatware from re-installing itself. Closes a firmware-level attack vector.
educationalContext.cons: Some vendor-specific features (anti-theft, driver helpers) may stop working.

name: Disable Multiplane Overlay (MPO)
description: Disables MPO which can cause black screens, flickering, and stuttering on some GPU/monitor combos.

educationalContext.howItWorks: MPO lets the GPU composite multiple planes (overlays) in hardware. Buggy implementations cause visual glitches.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes black screens, flickering, and stuttering in games on affected GPUs (esp. AMD pre-RDNA3).
educationalContext.cons: Slightly higher GPU power usage for desktop compositing. No impact on modern NVIDIA cards.

name: Remove Home from File Explorer
description: Removes the 'Home' page from File Explorer in Windows 11 and opens to 'This PC' instead.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets Explorer to open to This PC instead of the Home view that shows recent files and favorites.
educationalContext.pros: Faster access to drives. No privacy-leaking recent files shown on Explorer open.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the quick access to recent files and favorites on the Home page.

name: Disable Start Menu Recommendations & Suggestions
description: Removes app suggestions and recommendations from the Windows 11 Start Menu.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 shows 'Recommended' apps and tips in the Start Menu. This disables them.
educationalContext.pros: Cleaner Start Menu with no ads or suggestions. Faster Start Menu rendering.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see recently opened files or app suggestions in Start.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' -Name 'Start_TrackProgs' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Name 'HideRecommendedSection' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force

name: Set Time to UTC (Dual Boot Fix)
description: Makes Windows use UTC for the hardware clock — essential for dual-booting with Linux.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Linux uses UTC for the hardware clock, Windows uses local time. This makes both consistent.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes the time being wrong every time you switch between Linux and Windows.
educationalContext.cons: None if you're dual-booting. Slight confusion if you later remove Linux.

name: Disable App Launch Tracking
description: Stops Windows from tracking which apps you launch and how often.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows records every app launch to personalize Start Menu and search results. This stops that tracking.
educationalContext.pros: No more app launch history recorded by Windows.
educationalContext.cons: Start Menu 'Most used' apps section won't update.

name: Disable Feedback Requests
description: Stops Windows from asking for feedback and sending diagnostic feedback data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows periodically prompts you for feedback about your experience. This silences it.
educationalContext.pros: No more 'How likely are you to recommend Windows?' popups.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft won't receive your feedback about bugs or features.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'NumberOfSIUFInPeriod' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'PeriodInNanoSeconds' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Typing & Inking Data Collection
description: Stops Windows from collecting typing patterns and inking data to send to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows sends keyboard typing patterns and handwriting data to improve speech/ink recognition models.
educationalContext.pros: Your typing patterns and handwriting data stay completely private.
educationalContext.cons: Personalized autocorrect and ink recognition may be less accurate.

name: Disable App Camera Access
description: Denies all apps access to the camera by default.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Blocks UWP and desktop apps from accessing your camera through the Windows capability manager.
educationalContext.pros: No app can secretly use your webcam. LED should never light up unexpectedly.
educationalContext.cons: Video calls (Teams, Zoom, Discord) won't work until you re-enable per-app.

name: Disable App Contacts Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your Windows contacts.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Blocks apps from reading your contacts stored in the Windows People app.
educationalContext.pros: No app can harvest your contact list.
educationalContext.cons: Apps that integrate with Windows contacts (People, Mail) won't show contact info.

name: Disable App Calendar Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your calendar data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Blocks apps from reading your calendar events. Desktop apps that don't use the Windows API are unaffected.
educationalContext.pros: Your schedule and appointments stay private from third-party apps.
educationalContext.cons: UWP calendar integrations won't work.

name: Disable App Account Info Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your Microsoft account name, picture, and other info.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Blocks apps from reading your account name, picture, and email address.
educationalContext.pros: Apps can't see your real name, email, or account photo.
educationalContext.cons: Some apps may show a generic 'User' instead of your name.

name: Set Diagnostic Data to Minimum
description: Sets Windows diagnostic data collection to 'Required' (minimum) — the lowest level allowed.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects diagnostic data at Full or Required levels. This forces the minimum level.
educationalContext.pros: Minimizes telemetry sent to Microsoft to only what's required for security updates.
educationalContext.cons: Tailored experiences and optional diagnostic insights won't work.

name: Disable PowerShell 7 Telemetry
description: Sets the environment variable to disable PowerShell 7+ telemetry data collection.

educationalContext.howItWorks: PowerShell 7+ sends usage telemetry unless the POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT variable is set.
educationalContext.pros: No PowerShell usage data sent to Microsoft.
educationalContext.cons: None. This only affects telemetry, not functionality.

name: Disable Cross-Device Resume
description: Disables the cross-device activity resume feature in Windows 11 24H2+.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 24H2 can resume activities started on your phone or other devices. This disables it.
educationalContext.pros: No cross-device activity data shared with Microsoft servers.
educationalContext.cons: You can't resume mobile activities on your PC.

name: Set Unnecessary Services to Manual
description: Sets non-essential Windows services to Manual startup so they only run when needed.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Many Windows services auto-start but are rarely used. Setting them to Manual means they start on-demand.
educationalContext.pros: Faster boot, lower RAM usage, reduced background CPU activity.
educationalContext.cons: First use of some features may take a moment longer as the service starts.

execution.code: $services = @('DiagTrack','dmwappushservice','MapsBroker','lfsvc','SharedAccess','RemoteRegistry','RemoteAccess','TrkWks','WMPNetworkSvc','WSearch'); foreach ($s in $services) { if (Get-Service -Name $s -EA SilentlyContinue) { Set-Service -Name $s -StartupType Manual -EA SilentlyContinue } }; Set-Service -Name 'DiagTrack' -StartupType Disabled -EA SilentlyContinue; Stop-Service -Name 'DiagTrack' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: $services = @('DiagTrack','dmwappushservice','MapsBroker','TrkWks','WSearch'); foreach ($s in $services) { if (Get-Service -Name $s -EA SilentlyContinue) { Set-Service -Name $s -StartupType Automatic -EA SilentlyContinue } }

name: Disable Explorer Automatic Folder Discovery
description: Stops Explorer from auto-detecting folder types (Music, Pictures, etc.) which causes slow enumeration.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Explorer scans folder contents to decide the view template (Pictures, Music, etc.). This is slow for large folders.
educationalContext.pros: Dramatically faster folder opening, especially for game install dirs and large downloads.
educationalContext.cons: Folders always use the General template. No automatic Music/Pictures column layouts.

name: Disable Lock Screen Ads & Spotlight
description: Removes ads, tips, and Microsoft Spotlight from the lock screen.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows shows ads disguised as 'tips' and 'fun facts' on the lock screen. This disables all of them.
educationalContext.pros: Clean lock screen with no marketing content. Faster lock screen loading.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the rotating Bing wallpapers from Windows Spotlight.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'RotatingLockScreenOverlayEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338387Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338389Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Set DNS to Mullvad (100.64.0.4)
description: Sets DNS to Mullvad's privacy-focused DNS with ad/tracker blocking.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Mullvad DNS blocks ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level. No logs, no data collection.
educationalContext.pros: Privacy-first DNS with built-in ad/tracker blocking. No logging policy.
educationalContext.cons: Slower than Cloudflare for raw speed. Some sites may break if ads are blocked.

name: Set DNS to AdGuard (94.140.14.14)
description: Sets DNS to AdGuard's DNS which blocks ads, trackers, and phishing domains.

educationalContext.howItWorks: AdGuard DNS resolves normal domains but returns NXDOMAIN for known ad/tracker/malware domains.
educationalContext.pros: System-wide ad blocking at the DNS level. Blocks trackers and phishing sites.
educationalContext.cons: Some ad-supported sites or services may not work correctly.

name: Set DNS to Quad9 (9.9.9.9)
description: Sets DNS to Quad9 which provides malware blocking and privacy protection.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Quad9 uses threat intelligence from 40+ partners to block malicious domains at the DNS level.
educationalContext.pros: Strong malware domain blocking. Non-profit, privacy-focused. Swiss legal jurisdiction.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly slower than Cloudflare. Very aggressive blocking may rarely flag legitimate sites.

name: Set DNS to NextDNS (45.90.28.0)
description: Sets DNS to NextDNS — a customizable DNS with ad blocking, analytics, and parental controls.

educationalContext.howItWorks: NextDNS lets you customize your blocklists online. The default server provides basic protection.
educationalContext.pros: Highly customizable via nextdns.io dashboard. Excellent analytics and logging controls.
educationalContext.cons: Free tier limited to 300K queries/month. Full customization requires creating a NextDNS account.

name: Set DNS to OpenDNS / Cisco Umbrella (208.67.222.222)
description: Sets DNS to Cisco's OpenDNS with phishing/botnet protection and content filtering.

educationalContext.howItWorks: OpenDNS (now Cisco Umbrella) provides DNS-level security with enterprise-grade threat detection.
educationalContext.pros: Excellent phishing and botnet protection. Optional content filtering via OpenDNS account.
educationalContext.cons: Owned by Cisco — may log queries for analytics. Slightly slower in some regions.

name: Set DNS to Control D (76.76.2.0)
description: Sets DNS to Control D — a customizable DNS with ad blocking and geo-unblocking.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Control D provides DNS-level ad blocking and can unblock geo-restricted content.
educationalContext.pros: Ad blocking + geo-unblocking in one DNS. Very fast resolver infrastructure.
educationalContext.cons: Full features require a paid account. Free tier is basic DNS resolution.

name: Set DNS to Google (8.8.8.8)
description: Sets DNS to Google Public DNS — the most widely used public DNS resolver.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Google Public DNS is a fast, reliable DNS resolver. No ad blocking or filtering.
educationalContext.pros: Extremely fast and reliable. Massive global infrastructure with low latency.
educationalContext.cons: Google logs DNS queries (anonymized after 48h). No ad/tracker blocking.

name: Reset DNS to ISP Default (DHCP)
description: Resets DNS back to your ISP's default DNS servers via DHCP.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Removes any manually set DNS servers and lets your router/ISP assign DNS automatically via DHCP.
educationalContext.pros: Returns to default network configuration. Useful if a custom DNS is causing issues.
educationalContext.cons: Your ISP can see and log all your DNS queries. Usually slower than public DNS.
